question:
Why is nepotism generally frowned upon in a professional workplace?
A) It bypasses meritocracy and can lead to unqualified individuals holding power.
B) It forces families to spend too much time together.
C) It always results in the immediate bankruptcy of the company.
D) It guarantees that the business will be successful because family members trust each other.
